paused_reason: STDB schema change + spacetime publish + cross-binary coordination. Touches hexflo-coordination WASM, hex-nexus state port, hex-cli worker bootstrap. Resume deliberately, one phase at a time. The rogue-agent incident showed schema-touching changes need extra care.

tasks.title: Add worker_pool_intent table to hexflo-coordination: { id, role, desired_count, restart_strategy (permanent|transient|temporary), max_restarts, max_restart_window_secs, paused, created_at }. desired_count=0 + paused=true is how operators temporarily disable a pool.

Add worker_process table: { id, pool_id, role, host, pid, started_at, last_heartbeat, restart_count, in_crash_loop, exited_at, exit_reason }. Lifecycle owned by supervisor_tick reducer; agents emit heartbeats via existing agent_heartbeat reducer (extend to include restart_count read).
Add supervisor_event table: { id, ts, kind (spawn_request|crash_loop|escalation|restart_attempted), pool_id, worker_id?, payload }. Pure event log — subscribers consume.

phases.title: STDB schema — worker_pool_intent + worker_process + supervisor_event tables

tasks.title: Add #[reducer(scheduled = '10s')] supervisor_tick. For each non-paused worker_pool_intent row: count alive worker_process rows (last_heartbeat < now-45s, NOT in crash loop, exited_at is null) for that role. If alive < desired_count, emit supervisor_event{kind: spawn_request, pool_id}. If alive > desired_count, do nothing (excess workers stay until they die naturally).

Crash-loop detection inside supervisor_tick: for each worker_process where exited_at is set, increment restart_count on the pool. If a pool exceeds max_restarts within max_restart_window_secs, mark it in_crash_loop=true, emit supervisor_event{kind: crash_loop} + write to inbox priority 2 (operator override per ADR-060), STOP emitting spawn_request for that pool.

Restart strategy semantics: permanent (always respawn), transient (respawn on abnormal exit only — exit_reason != 'normal'), temporary (never respawn). Default permanent for hex-coder pool; temporary for one-shot tasks.

In hex-nexus, subscribe to the supervisor_event table. On kind=spawn_request, look up the pool's role. If host is local (or not specified), call existing spawn_local_agent() with the role. Record the spawned process_id in a new worker_process row.
On hex-agent process exit, the supervising nexus updates the worker_process row with exited_at + exit_reason. Wire into the existing agent_manager exit-handler.
On kind=crash_loop, write to operator inbox at priority 2 (overrides current work per ADR-060) so the operator sees 'pool X has crash-looped — paused after Y restarts in Z seconds'.
hex pool create <role> --count <N> [--restart permanent|transient|temporary] [--max-restarts N] [--restart-window SECS]. Calls worker_pool_intent_set reducer.
hex pool list (shows desired vs alive per pool, crash-loop status). hex pool pause/resume <id>. hex pool delete <id> (sets desired_count=0; supervisor stops respawning).
hex agent overview surfaces pool health. New row in the overview output: 'Worker pools: N defined, M crash-looped'.

phases.title: CLI surface — operators interact with pools

tasks.title: Add a SupervisorPanel to Brain showing each worker pool: role · desired/alive · restart count · crash-loop badge. Click to drill into the pool's worker_process rows. Crash-looped pools surfaced as decisions in the existing /api/decisions aggregator.

Today only the LOCAL nexus's hex-agent gets respawned. For real OTP-equivalent supervision across a fleet, each host's nexus subscribes to spawn_request events filtered by host_pool_assignment. A new table host_pool_assignment maps (pool_id, host_id) -> can_run_here. ADR-shaped change — write the ADR before implementation.

rationale: Today hex spawns a single local hex-agent that naively respawns on exit. There's no declarative 'I want N workers of role X', no crash-loop detection, no restart-strategy semantics, no cross-host supervision. Heartbeat cleanup marks dead agents but never replaces them. The result: '0 registered, 0 online' is a real outcome and the operator has no recovery without manual `hex agent worker --role X` invocations. Rebuilding this in the OTP supervisor pattern using STDB scheduled reducers gives declarative + transactional + restartable supervision without introducing Erlang.
title: STDB-backed worker supervisor — OTP-style restart strategies for hex-agent pools
